qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsigned wait resume endpoints allow unauthenticated execution resumption by numeric execution ID
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an authentication/signature bypass in the public waiting-webhook and waiting-form handlers (`packages/cli/src/webhooks/waiting-webhooks.ts` and `waiting-forms.ts`). GHSA-vpcf-gvg4-6qwr instead describes an authenticated expression-sandbox escape to host command execution, and the upstream fixes harden `packages/workflow/src/expression-sandboxing.ts`, `packages/workflow/src/expression.ts`, and `packages/workflow/src/extensions/expression-extension.ts` rather than the wait-resume webhook path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's expression-evaluation sandbox escape root cause. The only qualifying finding is an unauthenticated wait-resume webhook access-control flaw in a different n8n component.
evidence: GHSA-vpcf-gvg4-6qwr describes authenticated users with workflow creation or editing rights abusing crafted expressions to trigger host command execution.

The referenced upstream fixes (commits `9e5212ecbc5d2d4e6f340b636a5e84be6369882e` and `1479aab2d32fe0ee087f82b9038b1035c98be2f6`) harden the expression sandbox in `packages/workflow/src/expression-sandboxing.ts`, `packages/workflow/src/expression.ts`, and `packages/workflow/src/extensions/expression-extension.ts`, including blocking spread-based `process` access and other sandbox escapes.

The only valid `qualifying_vuln` finding in the session, `wait-resume-execution-id`, instead targets missing authentication/signature validation on `/webhook-waiting/:executionId` and `/form-waiting/:executionId` in `packages/cli/src/webhooks/waiting-webhooks.ts` and `packages/cli/src/webhooks/waiting-forms.ts`.
